Sacred Peaks Ranch Book 5

Drawing Her Close

Buy Now

About

She drove eleven hundred miles to be left alone. The woman with the wrench had other plans. An artist and a cowgirl collaborate on a mural, navigating their pasts and an unexpected connection. Described as the softest book in the series.

In This Book

  • 🎨 Ranch and small-town settings
  • 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Found family and ensemble warmth
  • 🌙 Slow intimacy over dramatic angst
  • 🌶️ Open door soft heat
  • 🏳️ Ace-spectrum representation
  • 💞 Age-gap romance (48 and 29)

Heat Level: 3.5/5 flames. One explicit scene.

Content Notes: Ace-spectrum representation; age gap; character with a complicated relationship to her body.

Book 5 in the series; can be read as a standalone.

Praise For This Book

"The Sacred land calls to the artists. This series of sapphic romances set on a ranch in Colorado has become one of my favorite reads."

— Goodreads Reviewer

"I loved the start being all about the scenery and the work done around the ranch."

— Goodreads Reviewer
